title: What is Volume Spread Analysis?
summary: VSA studies the relationship between volume, spread, close, and background to infer strength or weakness.
coreIdea: VSA studies the relationship between volume, spread, close, and background to infer strength or weakness.
steps: Identify volume relative to recent bars.
mistake: Do not treat this as a mechanical buy or sell signal. VSA is contextual.
practice: Open any chart, mark three recent bars, and write what volume, spread, close, and background suggest.

title: Volume, Spread, Close, Background
summary: The four pillars only become meaningful when read together, not as separate signals.
coreIdea: The four pillars only become meaningful when read together, not as separate signals.
steps: Identify volume relative to recent bars.
mistake: Do not treat this as a mechanical buy or sell signal. VSA is contextual.
practice: Open any chart, mark three recent bars, and write what volume, spread, close, and background suggest.

title: Effort vs Result
summary: Large effort with poor result often warns that the visible direction is being resisted.
coreIdea: Large effort with poor result often warns that the visible direction is being resisted.
steps: Identify volume relative to recent bars.
mistake: Do not treat this as a mechanical buy or sell signal. VSA is contextual.
practice: Open any chart, mark three recent bars, and write what volume, spread, close, and background suggest.

title: Wide Spread Bars
summary: Wide spread bars show expansion, but the close and background decide whether expansion is healthy or trapped.
coreIdea: Wide spread bars show expansion, but the close and background decide whether expansion is healthy or trapped.
steps: Identify volume relative to recent bars.
mistake: Do not treat this as a mechanical buy or sell signal. VSA is contextual.
practice: Open any chart, mark three recent bars, and write what volume, spread, close, and background suggest.

title: Narrow Spread Bars
summary: Narrow spread bars reveal lack of progress and often matter after prior effort.
coreIdea: Narrow spread bars reveal lack of progress and often matter after prior effort.
steps: Identify volume relative to recent bars.
mistake: Do not treat this as a mechanical buy or sell signal. VSA is contextual.
practice: Open any chart, mark three recent bars, and write what volume, spread, close, and background suggest.

title: Strong Background
summary: A strong background appears when selling pressure is absorbed and price refuses lower levels.
coreIdea: A strong background appears when selling pressure is absorbed and price refuses lower levels.
steps: Identify volume relative to recent bars.
mistake: Do not treat this as a mechanical buy or sell signal. VSA is contextual.
practice: Open any chart, mark three recent bars, and write what volume, spread, close, and background suggest.

title: Weak Background
summary: A weak background appears when rallies fail and supply repeatedly appears near value areas.
coreIdea: A weak background appears when rallies fail and supply repeatedly appears near value areas.
steps: Identify volume relative to recent bars.
mistake: Do not treat this as a mechanical buy or sell signal. VSA is contextual.
practice: Open any chart, mark three recent bars, and write what volume, spread, close, and background suggest.

title: No Demand
summary: A narrow up bar on low volume after weakness can show lack of professional buying.
coreIdea: A narrow up bar on low volume after weakness can show lack of professional buying.
steps: Identify volume relative to recent bars.
mistake: Do not treat this as a mechanical buy or sell signal. VSA is contextual.
practice: Open any chart, mark three recent bars, and write what volume, spread, close, and background suggest.

title: Upthrust
summary: An upthrust pushes above a level and closes weakly, often trapping breakout buyers.
coreIdea: An upthrust pushes above a level and closes weakly, often trapping breakout buyers.
steps: Identify volume relative to recent bars.
mistake: Do not treat this as a mechanical buy or sell signal. VSA is contextual.
practice: Open any chart, mark three recent bars, and write what volume, spread, close, and background suggest.

title: Buying Climax
summary: Very high volume after an extended rise may show late public buying meeting professional selling.
coreIdea: Very high volume after an extended rise may show late public buying meeting professional selling.
steps: Identify volume relative to recent bars.
mistake: Do not treat this as a mechanical buy or sell signal. VSA is contextual.
practice: Open any chart, mark three recent bars, and write what volume, spread, close, and background suggest.

title: Stopping Volume
summary: High volume after a fall with failure to continue lower can suggest selling is being absorbed.
coreIdea: High volume after a fall with failure to continue lower can suggest selling is being absorbed.
steps: Identify volume relative to recent bars.
mistake: Do not treat this as a mechanical buy or sell signal. VSA is contextual.
practice: Open any chart, mark three recent bars, and write what volume, spread, close, and background suggest.

title: No Supply
summary: A narrow down bar on low volume after strength can show lack of selling pressure.
coreIdea: A narrow down bar on low volume after strength can show lack of selling pressure.
steps: Identify volume relative to recent bars.
mistake: Do not treat this as a mechanical buy or sell signal. VSA is contextual.
practice: Open any chart, mark three recent bars, and write what volume, spread, close, and background suggest.

title: Shakeout
summary: A shakeout pushes below support and recovers, often removing weak holders before markup.
coreIdea: A shakeout pushes below support and recovers, often removing weak holders before markup.
steps: Identify volume relative to recent bars.
mistake: Do not treat this as a mechanical buy or sell signal. VSA is contextual.
practice: Open any chart, mark three recent bars, and write what volume, spread, close, and background suggest.

title: Successful Test
summary: A test checks whether supply remains; success requires low volume and follow-through.
coreIdea: A test checks whether supply remains; success requires low volume and follow-through.
steps: Identify volume relative to recent bars.
mistake: Do not treat this as a mechanical buy or sell signal. VSA is contextual.
practice: Open any chart, mark three recent bars, and write what volume, spread, close, and background suggest.

title: Failed Test
summary: A test fails when supply appears again and price cannot confirm strength.
coreIdea: A test fails when supply appears again and price cannot confirm strength.
steps: Identify volume relative to recent bars.
mistake: Do not treat this as a mechanical buy or sell signal. VSA is contextual.
practice: Open any chart, mark three recent bars, and write what volume, spread, close, and background suggest.

title: VSA Trade Thesis
summary: A valid thesis includes background, signal, confirmation, invalidation, and risk.
coreIdea: A valid thesis includes background, signal, confirmation, invalidation, and risk.

mistake: Do not treat this as a mechanical buy or sell signal. VSA is contextual.
